title: Building Shipwright
subtitle: The evidence-backed build register for the ship, its Computer, and the future Bridge.
status_note: Static verified snapshot. The Ship Computer is not built or connected; repository evidence and precursor collectors are named explicitly.

artifact.design: An implementation design exists; this is not runtime capability.
artifact.configured: A role, route, or slot is declared; execution is not proven.
artifact.built: Concrete code or a durable artifact exists.
artifact.accepted: The built artifact passed its named acceptance gate.

operational.not-applicable: This claim is documentary or structural rather than a running service.
operational.unmanned: A destination may exist, but no current occupant is expected to execute it.
operational.queue-only: A durable queue accepts work; wake, execution, and return are not automatic.
operational.standby: A runnable component exists but no fresh execution is currently established.
operational.live: Fresh, bounded execution evidence exists for the exact capability claimed.
operational.degraded: The capability operates with a named loss of coverage or assurance.
operational.unknown: Evidence is insufficient to establish current operating state.

evidence.configured: The claim comes from declared configuration only.
evidence.reported: An actor or document asserts the claim without independent confirmation.
evidence.receipt: A named system returned a checkable result.
evidence.verified: The claim passed the named tests or acceptance evidence.
evidence.unknown: Evidence is absent, stale, contradictory, or insufficient.

snapshot_metrics.label: Captain tools
snapshot_metrics.detail: Typed Bridge tools; raw Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Web, Agent, skills, and plugins absent.

snapshot_metrics.label: Captain unit tests
snapshot_metrics.detail: Fresh local run on 2026-07-11; all passed.

snapshot_metrics.label: Captain live bench
snapshot_metrics.detail: Historical fresh-session sample across Fable, Opus, and Sonnet; not an uptime guarantee.

snapshot_metrics.label: Bridge tests
snapshot_metrics.detail: 19 passed; one Chrome-dependent modal test skipped because Chrome did not start.

snapshot_metrics.label: Queued staff orders
snapshot_metrics.detail: Precursor collector snapshot; zero correlated reports. Queue receipt is not execution.

snapshot_metrics.label: Ship Computer
snapshot_metrics.detail: No service, canonical store, authenticated API, coverage registry, or SSE stream exists.

route_contracts.current: Meridian queue exists; synchronous loop is not built.
Target Captain/officer rollout is not built.

route_contracts.cadence: Bounded personal tooling and quick builds
route_contracts.current: Typed placeholder exists; manager and worker loop is unmanned.

route_contracts.cadence: Hours or days, then evidence-backed report
route_contracts.current: Interim No2 route is queue-only; full S3 is not built.

route_contracts.cadence: Ship engineering through the external Incubator PM
route_contracts.current: Chief Engineer is unmanned; cross-project delivery is not wired.

critical_path.name: Contracts and threat model
critical_path.gate: Ratified schemas, transition registry, identities, routes, truth classes, RBAC, retention, custody, and fixtures reject every orphan or ambiguous authority path.
critical_path.next: Create machine-readable schemas, OpenAPI, the field-level RBAC matrix, threat model, and v1 fixtures.

critical_path.name: Computer core and Captain vertical slice
critical_path.gate: One real Captain voyage completes authority, delivery, work, return, decision, redaction, chain verification, recovery, and external-state persistence.
critical_path.next: Build the external store, single writer, command ledger/outbox, query index, coverage service, authenticated API, and SSE.

critical_path.name: Orders, personal cells, Science, and gateways
critical_path.gate: A personal-cell voyage and a Science voyage remain distinct end to end, preserve root mission, costs, delivery, execution, report, evidence, and acceptance.
critical_path.next: Instrument typed transport, boards, Advisors, PAs, Science, models, tools, costs, supervisors, and standing-order runs.

critical_path.name: Workspace broker and factory route
critical_path.gate: Chief Engineer to Incubator PM to isolated factory work to reviewed Shipwright landing is traceable without direct manager access or main-copy writes.
critical_path.next: Design and build the single landing broker, workspace identities, handoff adapter, native jj/test/release events, and reconciliation.

critical_path.name: Hardening and external custody
critical_path.gate: An independent verifier accepts restored history and externally held checkpoint continuity after failure and compromise drills.
critical_path.next: Add signed external checkpoints, backup/restore, rotation, retention, disk-full, partition, load, and compromised-sensor drills.

critical_path.name: Bridge source-of-record cutover
critical_path.gate: Commodore acceptance, documented rollback, parallel-run parity, and zero unexplained coverage regressions.
critical_path.next: Run Computer and legacy collectors in parallel, reconcile discrepancies, then retire duplicate collectors deliberately.

bridge_readiness_gate.name: Minimum gate before a real Bridge prototype
bridge_readiness_gate.note: The former Falcon guardian command-deck concept belongs to the Shipwright Manual. The operational Bridge will be designed from scratch after this gate produces real contracts and a real voyage fixture.

requirements.label: Phase 0 schemas, identities, routes, transitions, truth classes, RBAC, and threat model ratified
External state root, canonical writer, command revision/outbox store, event chain, verifier, and rebuildable index running
One real directive-to-acceptance Captain voyage captured without repository telemetry writes
Authenticated status, snapshot, events, correlations, missions, work, deliveries, coverage, and stream contracts operating
Redaction, RBAC, replay, deduplication, crash recovery, and visible unknown/gap handling pass
The captured voyage becomes the versioned frontend fixture and acceptance baseline

groups.name: Keel and governance
groups.summary: The proven transport, first cell, spend floor, and remaining S1/S2 work.

claims.summary: Replacement prompt, complete built-in tool strip, typed MCP surface, model and effort pins, and subscription authentication were proven.
claims.next_gate: Retain as a regression fixture for every officer harness.

claims.summary: Contract, injection, crash/resume, and idempotency behavior ran against disposable stores without operational authority.
claims.next_gate: Keep the full adversarial suite in the officer release gate.

claims.capability: S1c Meridian and Advisor PA cell
claims.summary: Meridian's five-tool locked seat, separate Brain, PA drain, and first PA-sealed deliberation passed the named gate.
blockers: Off-runner Advisor cost data remains incomplete.
claims.next_gate: Wire a correlated wake, iterative follow-up, and answer-return loop without adding command authority.

claims.owner: Interim No1 / future Ops and Security
claims.summary: Grant registry, key inventory, cost ledger, roll-up, spend watch, and three named gates are built; the permanent Ops and Security seats remain unmanned.
blockers: Permanent officer custody and watch ownership are not staffed.
claims.next_gate: Transfer custody and monitoring through the S2 officer handoff without losing the single-writer floor.

claims.summary: The 12-plus-agent pinned sweep is specified and remains a gated assumption rather than proven crew capacity.
claims.next_gate: Run the pinned sweep and preserve per-seat evidence, failures, costs, and final adjudication.

claims.summary: The read-only observer through writer-token epoch ladder is designed; the new No1, Chief Engineer line, and repeatable kit are not built.
claims.next_gate: Complete every ladder rung while proving one board writer at every instant.

groups.name: Command seats and ship sections
groups.summary: Rank, role, runtime, and evidence remain separate; a configured seat is not a staffed seat.

claims.summary: Authenticated human flag authority and strategic command source.
claims.next_gate: Preserve authenticated directives and standing authority in the future Command Graph.

claims.summary: Three model-specific launchers plus the default alias boot a replacement-prompt Agent SDK runtime with 20 typed tools, private board, current, scratchpad, Brain, staff queues, provenance instrumentation, and bounded integrity-checked Captain Shipbook access.
blockers: Staff queues do not wake recipients automatically.
No instrumented Captain turn event stream has started.
claims.next_gate: Run a sustained instrumented watch with correlated staff returns and no false execution claims.

claims.summary: An interim resumable No1 and local floor records exist. The target locked Commander seat and writer-token handoff are S2 work.
blockers: Current-session liveness is not established by historical registry state.
claims.next_gate: Complete the S2 handoff and prove board authority, wake, report, and continuity.
claims.freshness: No fresh active turn at audit

claims.summary: An interim No2 route accepts broad commissions, but it does not auto-wake or return a correlated report; the full panel, council, and Ultra S3 department is not built.
claims.next_gate: Complete one hours-or-days Science voyage with sourced evidence, correlation, and Captain acceptance.

claims.summary: The reconnaissance pyramid and research corpus are designed; the S4 officer, fetch pipeline, and injection-safe provenance path are not built.
blockers: Fetch sanitation and source provenance require implementation.
claims.next_gate: Build and test an evidence-preserving reconnaissance voyage.

claims.summary: Security instruments exist through S-GOV, but the commissioned Security Officer is not staffed; interim custody remains outside the target seat.
blockers: Target officer harness and personal cell absent.
claims.next_gate: Staff the seat and transfer custody through an audited handoff.

claims.summary: Cost, roll-up, and watch instruments exist; the permanent Operations Officer and complete operational department are not staffed.
claims.next_gate: Staff the seat and prove cost, resource, and service operations during a live voyage.

claims.summary: The seat is explicitly deferred until the first sustained external-output mission; only chartered carve-outs exist now.
claims.next_gate: Stand up at or before the first sustained external-output mission and prove outbound review and provenance.

claims.summary: The seat is deferred behind staffed Operations and Security custody; no runtime capability is claimed.
claims.next_gate: Ratify a mission need and activate only after the custody gate is staffed.

claims.summary: A legacy resumable Doctor seat and current exist; the target S5 locked Counsellor with complete reach and intervention mechanics is not built.
blockers: Target S5 harness and reach mechanics absent.
claims.next_gate: Prove bounded intervention, records, escalation, and separation from CMO detection.
claims.freshness: No fresh active turn at audit

claims.summary: The system-health and observability role is designed separately from Counsellor treatment; the seat, privacy matrix, and complete observability surface do not exist.
claims.next_gate: Ratify privacy scope and prove detection against a real, permission-filtered event surface.

claims.summary: A typed destination exists, but no commissioned occupant or wake/report loop consumes it. The Chief Engineer is distinct from the Incubator PM.
claims.next_gate: Man the seat and prove one logged handoff and evidence-backed return through the distinct Incubator PM.

groups.name: Personal cells
groups.summary: Every Captain and commissioned officer eventually receives Thinking, Operations, and Engineering without confusing personal staff with ship departments.

claims.summary: Meridian's narrow locked seat is proven. The target direct conversational loop, own board, own scratchpad, and thinking-team orchestration are incomplete.
blockers: Consult does not wake or return synchronously.
Target Advisor board and thinking-team tools absent.
claims.next_gate: Complete a correlated multi-turn thought loop and preserve thread, cost, and answer receipt.

claims.summary: Opus, Fable, GLM, and Terra candidate slots are declared disabled. No slot funding, custom harness, web boundary, board, or live orchestration exists.
claims.next_gate: Ratify a bounded per-ship roster and prove specialization, parallel critique, judging, and synthesis within budget.

claims.summary: The Captain PA route is a target. Meridian's PA is a separate live precursor and does not prove the Captain/officer PA rollout.
blockers: No Captain PA occupant or operator team.
claims.next_gate: Prove one bounded research or operations request through the Captain PA and its team with receipts and Brain namespace isolation.

claims.summary: The fenced PA completed stage, ratify, Brain write, verbatim land, and seal; it is not the universal officer PA kit.
blockers: Interim permission fence remains migration debt.
claims.next_gate: Replace the interim fence with the final posture and retain fail-closed negative tests.

claims.summary: A typed placeholder route exists. No Lieutenant manager, officer-scoped board, isolated worker pool, review loop, or report return is live.
claims.next_gate: Build one bounded personal tool through a manager and isolated Ensign, then independently verify and return it.

claims.summary: Eleven Captain/officer cell declarations exist. No end-to-end repeatable kit, per-seat slot system, wake loop, namespace enforcement, or full rollout exists.
blockers: The Captain template itself is incomplete.
claims.next_gate: Finish and prove the Captain template, then roll out one officer at a time with negative cross-namespace tests.

groups.name: Engineering and factory route
groups.summary: Personal engineering, Shipwright's local floor, and the external Incubator are three distinct boundaries.

claims.summary: The repository has ten configured manager roles, worker types, boards, run records, panel artifacts, and a proven manager-worker pattern. Current activity must be established from fresh runner evidence, not configuration.
blockers: This floor is not the Chief Engineer and not the external Incubator PM.
claims.next_gate: Migrate native supervisor and board events into the Ship Computer without changing command boundaries.

claims.summary: The external project and its distinct PM/factory exist and are human-loadable. Shipwright has no commissioned automatic line into its managers.
blockers: No Shipwright-owned cross-project receipt or handoff adapter.
claims.next_gate: Prove a typed Chief Engineer handoff without direct Shipwright access to Incubator managers.

claims.capability: Chief Engineer to Incubator PM route
claims.summary: The command boundary and implementation package are specified. There is no live delivery, receipt, build return, acceptance report, or landing correlation.
claims.next_gate: Trace one real design package through PM acceptance, isolated build, evidence return, Chief Engineer review, and trusted landing.

claims.summary: The target forbids AI write access to the main copy and requires one isolated jj workspace per writer. Existing shared-working-copy and PA-sealing paths remain migration debt.
blockers: Policy is not enforced end to end.
Broker identity, custody, schema, failover, and negative tests are absent.
claims.next_gate: Build the broker and prove main-copy denial, serialized integration, cleanup, and recovery.

claims.summary: No design of record or implementation proves a single broker can validate scope, tests, evidence, change identity, serialization, and cleanup.
blockers: No schemas, queue, failover, or tests.
claims.next_gate: Ratify the design and run positive, negative, crash, and custody-loss fixtures.

groups.name: Command Graph and work flow
groups.summary: Authority, execution, and return are distinct planes; current queues are precursors, not the canonical graph.

claims.summary: Recipient-specific JSONL inboxes accept bounded envelopes and return IDs. At the audit snapshot, 35 orders were queued and none had a correlated report.
blockers: Latest report lookup is not envelope-addressed.
claims.next_gate: Move envelope creation, revision, delivery, ack, report, and acceptance into the transactional ledger.

claims.summary: No canonical directive, standing order, mission, objective, order, execution, return, and acceptance revision ledger exists.
claims.next_gate: Implement one primary parent, one Captain-owned root mission, typed transitions, immutable revisions, and lineage audits.

claims.summary: There is no versioned definition, occurrence key, trigger ledger, missed-trigger detection, duplicate suppression, or run outcome store.
claims.next_gate: Pass activation, hold, revocation, expiry, recurrence, duplicate, missed-trigger, and mission-attachment fixtures.

claims.capability: Rebuildable officer and department board projections
claims.summary: Current SQLite boards are advisory records. They are not projections from immutable command revisions and cannot create authority.
blockers: No ledger sequence, row digest, transactional outbox, or reconciliation.
claims.next_gate: Project open work into destination boards and detect missing, extra, and divergent rows.

groups.name: Ship Computer and API
groups.summary: Current repository collectors are migration precursors. Every canonical Computer component below is still a target.

claims.capability: Current Bridge normalizer and local API
claims.summary: The read-mostly local server normalizes repository ledgers, queues, boards, runner records, Brain mtimes, and topology into snapshot, event, order, actor, and trace views.
blockers: Two local POST routes remain outside the target command API.
claims.next_gate: Keep it behind a migration feature flag and compare against native Computer projections before retirement.
claims.freshness: 19 tests passed; one browser test skipped 2026-07-11

claims.summary: A separate read-only localhost API exposes board, VCS, manager, panel, and agent state with ETag polling and jailed document reads. It is not the Ship Computer API.
blockers: Repository-scoped collector, not canonical ship telemetry.
No Ship Computer identities, ingest, command transactions, or SSE.
claims.next_gate: Reuse bounded collectors and fixtures where valid; do not preserve its API as the target contract by accident.

claims.capability: External canonical event and command store
claims.summary: No external state root, append-only segments, command SQLite, query index, spool, quarantine, runtime registry, or service account exists.
blockers: Storage, retention, encryption, key custody, and migration contracts unimplemented.
claims.next_gate: Create mode-restricted external state and prove append, fsync, replay, index rebuild, and restore.

claims.capability: Canonical validator, redactor, deduplicator, and chain writer
claims.summary: The single accepted-observation writer and its deterministic append transaction are not implemented.
blockers: No service process, schema registry, checkpoint format, or startup verifier.
claims.next_gate: Pass duplicate, conflicting duplicate, crash-before/after-sync, invalid-head, and closed-segment fixtures.

claims.capability: Authenticated sensor ingestion and durable spools
claims.summary: No per-sensor credentials, ingest endpoint, producer policy, redaction intake, durable replay spool, backpressure, or quarantine operates.
blockers: Sensor inventory and credential plan not ratified.
claims.next_gate: Instrument the Captain first, then prove at-least-once replay and fail-closed side effects under Computer outage.

claims.capability: Authenticated query, command, and ingest API
claims.summary: The `/api/v1` status, snapshot, event, correlation, actor, mission, standing order, work, delivery, task, workspace, cost, coverage, command transaction, and ingest contracts are design only.
blockers: No OpenAPI, identity broker, authorization engine, or service.
claims.next_gate: Generate a machine-readable contract and pass field-scope, cursor-binding, idempotency, and cross-capability denial tests.

claims.summary: There is no accepted-sequence SSE channel, authorized compact delta, resume window, snapshot-required fallback, or degraded polling state.
blockers: Canonical accepted sequence and query authorization absent.
claims.next_gate: Prove resume, scope filtering, heartbeat semantics, stream expiry, and bounded polling fallback.

claims.summary: No authoritative registry identifies expected sensors, owners, last-seen sequence, lag, failure, exclusions, or blind spots.
blockers: Required inventory and freshness policies not machine-readable.
claims.next_gate: Make every expected sensor and every intentional exclusion visible, testable, and queryable.

claims.summary: Initial role profiles and redaction rules are written. The field matrix, credentials, policy engine, secret corpus, privacy decisions, and adversarial proof are not ratified or built.
blockers: Commodore, Captain, Security, CMO, and personal-cognition field scopes remain unresolved.
claims.next_gate: Ratify exact fields per capability and prove secrets and narrower-role content never reach store, response, log, or HTML.

groups.name: Provenance and observable truth
groups.summary: Evidence classes must stay visible; recorded bytes do not prove every underlying claim.

claims.capability: Captain observable turn and tool provenance
claims.summary: Mode-restricted, redacted, correlated, hash-chained provenance code and tests exist for turns, providers, tools, completion, refusal, and uncertain audit failure.
blockers: No real `events.jsonl` has been produced by an instrumented Captain turn.
claims.next_gate: Run the next live Captain watch through instrumentation and verify the resulting chain independently.

claims.summary: The expected `project-management/bridge/captain/events.jsonl` file is absent; chain status is not-started and historical activity is not backfilled.
blockers: No instrumented live turn has emitted the file.
claims.next_gate: Capture one full turn and show valid count, head hash, correlations, redactions, and coverage age.

claims.summary: The current collector labels recorded, derived, and configured events across queues, runner artifacts, panels, costs, Brain mtimes, and state writes. It is useful but incomplete and mutable.
claims.next_gate: Treat each collector as a versioned migration sensor and replace it with native emitters after parity tests.

claims.summary: No external checkpoint custody prevents a fully compromised local service account from rewriting retained history.
blockers: Custodian, signing keys, publication cadence, and verifier unratified.
claims.next_gate: Publish and restore across an externally held chain head, then verify continuity independently.

groups.name: Manuals and role knowledge
groups.summary: The human, Captain, and department views must share facts without sharing the same reading depth.

claims.summary: A consolidated Markdown manual and interactive HTML companion exist, covering command, seats, personal cells, factory, access, provenance, Computer, graph, readiness, and the complete Captain/officer manual library.
blockers: The HTML companion is hand-maintained and can drift from the Markdown source and generated build register.
claims.next_gate: Add a structural drift check for the HTML companion while keeping the build register and Shipbook checks in every documentation acceptance path.

claims.summary: This generated page reports the full build without pretending it is live Ship Computer telemetry or assigning a fake overall percentage.
blockers: It remains a static snapshot until native Computer readiness projections exist.
claims.next_gate: Regenerate on accepted build changes; later consume authenticated Computer readiness projections.

claims.summary: The generated Captain volume embeds the canonical whole-ship manual, shared officer operating standard, and all ten department books byte-for-byte. The Captain harness exposes its verified index and enum-bounded whole sections through read-only ship.knowledge without raw path access.
blockers: The generated volume must be rerun whenever an embedded source changes.
claims.next_gate: Run the deterministic shipbook freshness check in every manual-affecting acceptance path.

claims.summary: The shared officer operating standard teaches rank, authority, routing, personal Thinking/Operations/Engineering cells, boards, evidence duties, failure procedure, and the distinction between documented routes and runtime enforcement.
blockers: Most officer endpoints and routes remain target or configured rather than runtime-enforced.
claims.next_gate: Update the operating standard alongside each ratified runtime route or authority change.

claims.summary: Ten atomic department books cover mission, seats, command boundaries, exact officer contacts, personal cells, tools, boards, costs, provenance, failure doctrine, and current-versus-target state.
blockers: Most departments are not yet manned or fully implemented; the books describe those gaps explicitly.
claims.next_gate: Require each department build slice to update its source book and pass deterministic Captain-volume assembly.

groups.name: Bridge and future command surfaces
groups.summary: The Manual owns the commissioned Falcon imagery. The operational Bridge starts again from real data and a real API.

claims.summary: The cinematic Falcon guardian and guardian command-deck direction are assigned to the Shipwright Manual and its editorial/building surfaces, not the operational Bridge.
claims.next_gate: Keep manual and operational Bridge identities distinct as both evolve.

claims.summary: The operational Bridge requires a complete from-scratch product and visual design. It will not reuse the Manual page as its shell or treat fixture data as live.
blockers: Minimum Bridge readiness gate is entirely unmet.
claims.next_gate: After the minimum gate passes, design desktop and mobile operational flows against the real API and evidence semantics.

claims.summary: Falcon Command is reserved for a later fleet-level headquarters with a Command Computer, every ship Bridge, cross-ship visibility, and ship communications. It is not the Shipwright Bridge.
blockers: No fleet-level product or Command Computer design is commissioned in this build.
claims.next_gate: Preserve the name and boundary; design only after at least one Ship Computer-backed Bridge is operational.

known_register_issues.title: Ship Computer board epic absent
known_register_issues.detail: The design index names a `ship-computer` epic, but no such board item exists. Implementation must not begin as untracked work.

known_register_issues.title: Bridge naming conflict on the board
known_register_issues.detail: The `bridge-buildout` epic still assigns cross-ship fleet visibility to Shipwright's Bridge. The newer boundary reserves that product for future Falcon Command. Supersede or amend the board record before using it.

known_register_issues.title: Broken board evidence paths
known_register_issues.detail: `bridge-buildout` and `activity-feed-board-source` point to missing active design documents; `ship-org-sgov` points to its old path instead of `docs/design/implemented/ship-org-sgov.md`.

known_register_issues.title: Historical build-state copy drifts
known_register_issues.detail: The older build-state page reports 31 Captain tests and historical RUNNING seats. Fresh verification is 37 tests, and runtime state must come from fresh evidence rather than static labels.

known_register_issues.title: Queued work has no correlated return
known_register_issues.detail: The precursor snapshot found 35 queued staff orders and zero correlated reports. Do not interpret order volume as execution capacity.

known_register_issues.title: Observability remains bounded by mutable sources
known_register_issues.detail: Current repository collectors are useful migration evidence, but there is no authenticated producer identity, immutable accepted sequence, complete coverage registry, or external checkpoint custody.

source_priority: Accepted Ship Computer records and receipts, once the Computer exists
Named acceptance tests, sealed artifacts, and independently checkable receipts
Durable source-boundary records with explicit truth class
